mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month increase in FEDFUNDS reflects the onset of a Fed tightening cycle that raises the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as valuations compress ahead of realized hikes.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.4% just prior to first 2022 rate hike cycle
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS momentum and forward 20d returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or returns following FEDFUNDS increases are not significantly negative (p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE falls below 5.0% while remaining near multi-decade lows, labor market tightness raises wage-inflation expectations, prompting a more aggressive anticipated Fed response that depresses equity valuations over the following quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.9% as of latest snapshot
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following UNRATE readings below 5.0% are positive in more than 55% of historical instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Robust RSAFS growth combined with rising FEDFUNDS indicates consumer demand strong enough to tolerate near-term rate hikes without triggering recession fears, producing positive 20-day forward S&P 500 returns as the market prices resilient earnings alongside a credible tightening path.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month annualized growth > 5% AND FEDFUNDS rising
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 456321 million as of latest month
regime_hint: early monetary tightening with resilient consumer demand
failure_condition: Joint trigger instances show negative or statistically insignificant mean 20d forward returns (t-test p>0.05) across a 3-year rolling out-of-sample window.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening regime, continued INDPRO expansion signals that industrial supply capacity is keeping pace with demand, reducing supply-shock inflation risk and supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter as margin pressure fears ease.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 104.40 as of latest month
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with expanding industrial production
failure_condition: During tightening regimes, INDPRO expansion periods show 60d forward returns not significantly different from zero or negative (p>0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation crosses above 7%, markets price in accelerated and larger Fed rate hikes within days, causing an immediate risk-off repricing that produces negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 239.828 implying elevated YoY inflation near decision date
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following CPI YoY prints above 7% are positive in more than 55% of historical instances since 2021, or Rank IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in HOUST over multiple months signals weakening interest-rate-sensitive demand and an early warning of broader economic slowdown, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter as forward earnings expectations are revised down.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1211 thousand units as of latest snapshot
failure_condition: Rank IC between 3-month HOUST decline and 60d forward returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window, or returns following HOUST declines are not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strong PAYEMS growth and falling UNRATE alongside rising FEDFUNDS signal an overheating labor market that pressures the Fed toward faster tightening, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month as rate-hike expectations are repriced upward.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month growth > 400k AND UNRATE falling AND FEDFUNDS rising
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 144598 thousand persons as of latest month
regime_hint: overheating labor market during tightening onset
failure_condition: Joint trigger instances yield mean 20d forward returns not significantly negative (p>0.05) across a 3-year rolling out-of-sample window.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL-implied inflation substantially exceeds FEDFUNDS, real policy rates remain deeply negative, providing an effective subsidy to risk assets and supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter until the real rate gap narrows meaningfully.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.4% far below CPI-implied inflation
failure_condition: 60d forward returns during periods of real rate (FEDFUNDS minus CPI YoY) below -3% are not significantly positive (p>0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.
